name: Hoppin' John (Craig Claiborne)
recipeIngredient: 1/8 pound streaky bacon or salt pork, cut into small cubes (about 1/2 cup)
10 ounces fresh or frozen black-eyed peas
1/4 teaspoon dried hot red pepper flakes
recipeInstructions:
Garnishes
1 ripe tomato -- cored
1/4 pound sharp cheddar cheese -- finely grated
1 cup scallion (including green part) -- finely chopped
1 Cook the bacon or salt pork in a saucepan, stirring often, until all cubes are crisp. Add carrots, celery, and onion; cook, stirring, about 1 minute.
2 Stir in peas, garlic, about 1-1/4 cups water (or to barely cover), thyme, bay leaf, salt, and red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil, then simmer, uncovered, 30 to 40 minutes until tender but not mushy.
3 Remove from heat. In a separate saucepan, combine rice with 1-1/2 cups water and bring to a boil; simmer, covered, about 17 minutes. Stir in butter (or cook rice as you normally would).
4 Cut the tomato into 1/4-inch cubes (about 1 cup). Spoon hot pea mixture over rice, sprinkle cheese over peas, arrange tomato cubes around rice, and scatter scallions over tomatoes.
5 Serve immediately.
